message:
test(readme): JsonOrient round-trip across all 5 variants

JsonOrient has 5 pandas-parity variants (Records, Columns, Index,
Split, Values). Existing tests only exercised Records — a regression
in any of the other 4 orient modes would not have been caught.

Adds readme_json_orient_round_trip — reachable via prelude alone —
that loops over all 5 variants and for each:

- write_json_string(&df, orient) → non-empty JSON
- read_json_str(&json, orient) → DataFrame with row count preserved

Closes frankenpandas-fd90.27.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankenpandas-fd90.24","title":"test(readme): non-default CsvReadOptions coverage (delimiter / no-header / na_values / usecols / nrows / skiprows)","description":"CsvReadOptions has 10+ pandas-parity fields (delimiter, has_headers, na_values, keep_default_na, na_filter, index_col, usecols, nrows, skiprows, dtype, parse_dates) but the existing readme_quick_example tests only use ::default(). A regression in any of these fields would not surface.

Plan: add readme_csv_options_round_trip exercising via prelude alone:

- Custom delimiter (semicolon-separated)
- has_headers=false (column names auto-generated as 0, 1, ...)
- na_values custom marker (e.g. 'MISSING' should round-trip to Null)
- usecols (column subset)
- nrows (truncated read)
- skiprows (skip preamble)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T12:33:55.745179646Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T12:36:33.088157501Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T12:36:33.086023457Z","close_reason":"completed: 6 CsvReadOptions fields covered","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-fd90.24","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-fd90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-27T12:33:55.745179646Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-fd90.25","title":"test(readme): non-default CsvWriteOptions coverage (delimiter / na_rep / header / include_index / index_label)","description":"Sister to fd90.24 (CsvReadOptions). CsvWriteOptions has 5 pandas-parity fields (delimiter, na_rep, header, include_index, index_label). Both the struct and write_csv_string_with_options are in the prelude (since fd90.216) but have ZERO integration test coverage.

Plan: add readme_csv_write_options_round_trip exercising via prelude alone:
- delimiter = b';' (output uses ; separator)
- na_rep = 'NA' (Null cells render as 'NA' string, not empty)
- header = false (data only, no header line)
- include_index = true (index becomes first column)
- index_label = Some('row_id')","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T12:37:24.353156832Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T12:39:06.077982040Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T12:39:06.075842085Z","close_reason":"completed: 4 CsvWriteOptions fields covered","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-fd90.25","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-fd90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-27T12:37:24.353156832Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-fd90.26","title":"test(readme): non-default ExcelReadOptions / ExcelWriteOptions coverage","description":"Sister to fd90.24/25 (CSV options). ExcelReadOptions has 6 fields (sheet_name, has_headers, usecols, names, index_col, skip_rows) and ExcelWriteOptions has 4 (sheet_name, index, index_label, header). Both have been in the prelude since fd90.207/216 but only ::default() is exercised by integration tests.

Plan: add readme_excel_options_round_trip:
- Excel write with custom sheet_name, then read with matching sheet_name
- Excel write with index=true + index_label
- Excel read with usecols / names / skip_rows","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T12:39:52.669267186Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T12:41:59.445554540Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T12:41:59.443292454Z","close_reason":"completed: ExcelReadOptions/ExcelWriteOptions covered","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-fd90.26","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-fd90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-27T12:39:52.669267186Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-fd90.27","title":"test(readme): JsonOrient round-trip across all 5 variants (Records / Columns / Index / Split / Values)","description":"JsonOrient has 5 pandas-parity variants: Records, Columns, Index, Split, Values. Existing tests only exercise Records. The other 4 variants are documented (pandas pd.read_json supports all 5 orient modes) but a regression in any of them would not be caught.

Plan: add readme_json_orient_round_trip exercising all 5 orient modes:
- For each: write_json_string(&df, orient) → read_json_str(&json, orient) → assert shape","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T12:42:50.282519052Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T12:43:25.551960356Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-fd90.27","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-fd90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-27T12:42:50.282519052Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-fd90.3","title":"fp-io read_sql_table_columns chunksize helper","description":"Child slice of frankenpandas-fd90: add read_sql_table_columns chunk helper for pandas-style read_sql_table(..., columns=[...], chunksize=...) on the existing SqlConnection surface.  {"id":"frankenpandas-fd90.4","title":"fp-io SQL chunks index_col helpers","description":"Child slice of frankenpandas-fd90: add chunked SQL read helpers that combine pandas-style chunksize with index_col promotion. Cover read_sql/read_sql_query/read_sql_table chunk paths by wrapping SqlChunkIterator, preserving per-chunk RangeIndex when index_col is None and promoting/removing the named column when set.
